GraceRequirements.Annotation: Portable OpenCode workflow package with plugins and a Bun CLI for install, sync, semantic model-role assignment, controller-led workflow routing, safer editing, secrets redaction, and cross-device setup.
GraceRequirements.Keywords: opencode, plugins, workflow, model-roles, hashline-edit, work-items, controller, bun, typescript

SourceOfTruth.LegacyState: docs/*.xml are GRACE 3 migration sources only and are not current GRACE 4 state.

Users.User: Engineer who wants a portable OpenCode workflow across devices, providers, and repositories without hardcoding provider-specific model IDs into shared agents, subagents, skills, or commands.
Maintainer who needs deterministic package, CLI, plugin, workflow, and release behavior for @osovv/vv-opencode.

Goals.Goal: Register and sync vvoc-owned OpenCode plugin, agent, skill, and config surfaces safely through the Bun CLI binary named vvoc.
Keep shared workflow definitions portable by using semantic vv-role:* model references and resolving them from the selected canonical vvoc config at runtime.
Make multi-agent work explicit through work items, tracked subagent headers, strict result protocol fields, reviewer result collection, hard stops, and bounded review rounds.
Reduce long-run risk through Guardian permission review, secrets redaction, safer hash-anchored edits, and conservative config mutation.
Keep specs, plans, prompts, skills, config, graph, and verification artifacts grep-friendly and deterministic.

UC-001.Action: User runs vvoc install for global or project scope.
UC-001.Goal: Register @osovv/vv-opencode in OpenCode config, seed canonical vvoc config, set vv-controller as managed default, install managed agents and skills, and write role-reference based model fields.
UC-001.Acceptance: The pinned package appears in OpenCode plugin config, vvoc.json is created or safely refreshed, built-in roles and presets are seeded, default_agent is vv-controller, managed skills are installed, and model-bearing fields use vv-role:* references.

UC-002.Action: User runs vvoc sync after updates or on another machine.
UC-002.Goal: Refresh vvoc-owned config, prompts, skills, role presets, default controller selection, and current managed OpenCode registrations without repairing invalid legacy configs.
UC-002.Acceptance: Valid current schema v3 config is rewritten canonically while role assignments and plugin toggles are preserved; invalid or old configs fail before rewrite; old pre-rename entries are left untouched.

UC-005.Action: Provider-bound chat or tool payloads contain secrets while SecretsRedactionPlugin is active.
UC-005.Goal: Prevent raw secrets from being sent to LLM providers while restoring placeholders where local runtime needs originals.
UC-005.Acceptance: Secrets are replaced before provider-bound requests, restored after completions and before tool execution, and stable for stable configured secrets.

UC-006.Action: A primary chat session starts with vvoc installed.
UC-006.Goal: Inject reusable main-session guidance while avoiding prompt pollution for known subagents.
UC-006.Acceptance: Eligible primary agents including vv-controller receive guidance once; known subagents and helper agents do not inherit it.

UC-007.Action: User runs vvoc preset list, show, or apply.
UC-007.Goal: Inspect and apply declarative role presets in canonical vvoc.json without rewriting OpenCode target fields directly.
UC-007.Acceptance: Only roles listed by the selected preset are changed, unlisted roles remain unchanged, and OpenCode config stays role-reference based.

UC-008.Action: User runs vvoc role list, set, or unset.
UC-008.Goal: Manage built-in and user-defined semantic roles referenced as vv-role:<role-id>.
UC-008.Acceptance: Built-in roles are visible, valid custom roles can be added, model selections normalize consistently, and built-in roles cannot be removed accidentally.

UC-009.Action: OpenCode config uses model: vv-role:<role-id> in supported fields.
UC-009.Goal: Resolve shared semantic model intent to user-local concrete provider/model selections at OpenCode startup.
UC-009.Acceptance: Supported fields resolve to concrete models, variant-bearing assignments are split where safe, and unresolved roles fail loudly without fallback.

UC-010.Action: Controller calls work_item_open, work_item_list, or work_item_close.
UC-010.Goal: Bind tracked subagent launches to explicit work-item identity, mode, required reviewers, lifecycle, and review-round metadata.
UC-010.Acceptance: Open calls require idempotent keys, title, mode, and requiredReviewers; list returns lifecycle metadata; close succeeds only from ready_to_close.

UC-011.Action: Main session launches vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, or vv-code-reviewer through task.
UC-011.Goal: Enforce valid work-item headers, open work-item identity, stored mode, current review-round policy, and implementation-mode review limits.
UC-011.Acceptance: Missing, malformed, unknown, invalid transition, duplicate reviewer, after-hard-stop, and round-overflow launches are rejected; untracked launches pass through.

UC-012.Goal: Parse strict top-block protocol fields, validate identity and status, update workflow state, and halt on protocol errors or hard stops.
UC-012.Acceptance: Reviewer results are collected per round, review-only FAIL is a completed finding result, NEEDS_CONTEXT and BLOCKED halt workflow, malformed results fail closed, and counters increment deterministically.

UC-013.Action: A tracked review cycle repeats after implementation fixes.
UC-013.Goal: Cap repeated review churn with explicit counters keyed by work item.
UC-013.Acceptance: Rounds one and two are allowed, round three is rejected with explicit next-action guidance, and no semantic heuristics are used.

UC-014.Action: Main session starts with WorkflowPlugin active.
UC-014.Goal: Inject workflow protocol guidance into eligible primary sessions.
UC-014.Acceptance: vv-controller receives guidance for work items, headers, hard-stop handling, state inspection, and loop gates; helper primaries and known subagents are skipped.

UC-015.Action: User disables secrets-redaction through canonical vvoc config or vvoc plugin disable.
UC-015.Goal: Make the plugin return empty hooks on the next session so raw payloads reach the provider intentionally.
UC-015.Acceptance: plugins["secrets-redaction"] is false, the plugin factory reads it, and other plugins continue to load normally.

UC-016.Goal: Fall back to OpenCode default edit behavior when hashline-edit causes regressions.
UC-016.Acceptance: plugins["hashline-edit"] false makes HashlineEditPlugin return empty hooks and leaves OpenCode default edit available.

UC-017.Action: User enables Guardian through vvoc plugin enable guardian.
UC-017.Goal: Set plugins.guardian true and re-enable Guardian on next session.
UC-017.Acceptance: The CLI validates the plugin name, writes true, and prints restart guidance.

UC-018.Action: User syncs while plugins are disabled.
UC-018.Goal: Refresh managed config without resetting plugin toggles.
UC-018.Acceptance: Disabled plugins stay disabled and enabled plugins stay enabled after sync.

UC-019.Goal: See OpenCode-level plugin registration and vvoc-level enabled or disabled toggle status.
UC-019.Acceptance: Output includes separate vvoc status based on canonical vvoc.json plugins section.

UC-020.Action: User runs vvoc upgrade with old or invalid existing vvoc config.
UC-020.Goal: Finish package installation while making post-install sync failure explicit and recoverable by manual config repair.
UC-020.Acceptance: The package install can succeed, sync failure is reported as partial upgrade, config remains unchanged, and user is instructed to fix vvoc.json manually.

NonGoals.NonGoal: No backward compatibility or automatic migration path for pre-role vvoc schemas after schema v3.
No target-based model override UX after the role-based cutover.
No semantic task grouping, embeddings, n-grams, or fuzzy work-item identity in workflow v1.
No workflow tracking for investigator, guardian, or enhancer in workflow v1.
No per-plugin toggle support beyond the vvoc-managed plugin set.
No silent cleanup of old pre-rename managed-agent or command entries during sync.

Constraints.Constraint: OpenCode config remains in OpenCode-managed paths; vvoc-managed settings live in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json or project-local .vvoc where supported.
Persisted vvoc data lives under $XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc, not OpenCode config.
Config writes must be conservative, idempotent, strict on current schema v3, and comment-preserving where practical.
Role resolution fails loudly for unknown roles.
Workflow enforcement uses explicit IDs and simple counters only.
Tracked subagent names follow vv-* naming: vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer.
vv-spec and vv-plan skills write durable spec packages under project-local .vvoc/specs/<id>.
Guardian uses current OpenCode permission reply paths only.
Hashline edit validation accepts only current line and context hash anchors.

Risks.Risk: Shared workflows may reference custom roles not assigned on another machine, causing startup failures.
Unsafe rewrites of user-maintained config could damage trust in the CLI.
OpenCode plugin and SDK surface area may evolve.
Provider alias models may be required for some role assignments.
LLMs may produce malformed tracked subagent top-block protocol output.
In-memory or persisted workflow state can confuse users after process or session transitions if diagnostics are weak.
Forcing default_agent to vv-controller may surprise users who intentionally selected another default.

OpenQuestions.Question: Should future versions validate role assignments against provider-declared capabilities such as vision?
Should diagnostics discover literal provider/model strings still present in OpenCode config fields?
Should investigator become a tracked agent in a future workflow version?
